message:
feat(core): implement pack contracts system for customizable skill packing

Introduces a comprehensive pack contracts system that allows users to define
custom packing strategies with fine-grained control over which skill sections
are included and how they are weighted during token-constrained packing.

New files:
- src/core/pack_contracts.rs: Complete contract system implementation
  - 6 built-in presets: Complete, Debug, Refactor, Learn, QuickRef, CodeGen
  - Custom contract persistence via contracts.json
  - Group-level and tag-level weight overrides
  - Required groups that bypass budget constraints
  - Validation and preset-from-name resolution

- src/cli/commands/contract.rs: CLI interface for contract management
  - `contract create`: Interactive contract creation with preset inheritance
  - `contract list`: Display available contracts (built-in + custom)

Modified files:
- src/core/skill.rs: Extended PackContract struct with:
  - group_weights: HashMap<String, f32> for section group multipliers
  - tag_weights: HashMap<String, f32> for tag-based multipliers
  - section_title: Option<String> on slices for group identification

- src/core/packing.rs: Contract-aware scoring algorithm
  - score_slice_with_contract() applies group/tag multipliers
  - contract_weight() resolves hierarchical weight overrides
  - Required groups guaranteed inclusion regardless of budget

- src/core/disclosure.rs: TokenBudget gains contract field
  - render_packed_body() respects contract constraints
  - Contract-aware progressive disclosure

- src/core/slicing.rs: section_title propagation to slices
- src/core/mod.rs: Export pack_contracts module
- src/cli/commands/load.rs: --contract flag for load command

Use cases:
- Debug contract prioritizes examples, errors, edge_cases sections
- Refactor contract emphasizes structure, api, internals
- QuickRef contract maximizes information density for reference
- CodeGen contract focuses on templates, patterns, examples
- Custom contracts for domain-specific packing strategies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
